title: Daily Routines
shortSummary: Describe your daily habits — waking up, meals, work, and bedtime. Introduces reflexive verbs and time expressions.
culturalNote: Daily schedules in Spain and Latin America differ markedly from Anglo countries. Spaniards typically eat lunch between 2-3 PM, take a break in the afternoon, and have dinner around 9-10 PM. The 'sobremesa' — lingering at the table chatting after a meal — is a cherished tradition. Work hours often extend later into the evening to accommodate this rhythm.

keyPhrases.context: Reflexive verb — you wake yourself up
Desayunar = to have breakfast (no reflexive needed)

keyPhrases.english: I go to work by metro
I work from nine to five

keyPhrases.context: Main meal in Spain is lunch, typically 2-3 PM

keyPhrases.english: I go to bed at eleven
keyPhrases.context: Reflexive: acostarse = to put oneself to bed

keyPhrases.spanish: Los fines de semana duermo hasta tarde
keyPhrases.pronunciation: lohs FEE-nehs deh seh-MAH-nah DWEHR-moh AHS-tah TAR-deh

keyPhrases.english: I always eat with my family
keyPhrases.context: Frequency adverbs go before the verb

grammar.explanation: Reflexive verbs describe actions you do to yourself. The pronoun (me, te, se, nos, os, se) goes before the conjugated verb. Many daily routine verbs are reflexive: despertarse, ducharse, vestirse, acostarse.
grammar.commonMistake: Forgetting the reflexive pronoun: 'levanto a las siete' is incomplete — you need 'me levanto'.

grammar.rule: Time expressions: antes de / después de
grammar.explanation: Use 'antes de' (before) and 'después de' (after) + infinitive or noun to sequence your activities. 'Antes de comer' (before eating), 'después del trabajo' (after work).

examples.es: Después de cenar, leo un libro
examples.en: After dinner, I read a book

grammar.commonMistake: Using a conjugated verb after 'antes de' — say 'antes de comer' (infinitive), not 'antes de como'.

grammar.rule: Frequency adverbs: siempre, nunca, a veces
grammar.explanation: Common frequency adverbs: siempre (always), casi siempre (almost always), a veces (sometimes), casi nunca (almost never), nunca (never). They typically go before the verb.

examples.en: I always have coffee for breakfast

examples.es: Nunca me acuesto antes de las diez
examples.en: I never go to bed before ten

examples.es: A veces como en un restaurante
examples.en: Sometimes I eat at a restaurant

grammar.commonMistake: Double negation confusion: 'nunca' already means never, but in Spanish you CAN say 'no como nunca' — double negatives reinforce each other.

dialogue.setup: Two friends, Lucía and David, compare their daily routines over coffee.

lines.speaker: Lucía
lines.english: What time do you normally get up?

lines.speaker: David
lines.spanish: Me levanto a las seis y media. Soy muy madrugador.
lines.english: I get up at 6:30. I'm an early riser.

lines.speaker: Lucía
lines.spanish: ¡Qué temprano! Yo me despierto a las ocho.
lines.english: So early! I wake up at eight.

lines.speaker: David
lines.english: Do you have breakfast at home?

lines.speaker: Lucía
lines.spanish: Sí, siempre desayuno tostadas y café.
lines.english: Yes, I always have toast and coffee for breakfast.

lines.speaker: David
lines.spanish: Yo nunca desayuno en casa. Como algo en la oficina.
lines.english: I never have breakfast at home. I eat something at the office.

lines.speaker: Lucía
lines.english: And after work, what do you do?

lines.speaker: David
lines.spanish: Voy al gimnasio y después ceno sobre las nueve.
lines.english: I go to the gym and then have dinner around nine.

quiz.question: How do you say 'I wake up at seven' in Spanish?
quiz.explanation: 'Despertarse' is reflexive — 'me despierto' means 'I wake (myself) up'.

quiz.question: _____ levanto a las ocho. (I get up at eight)
quiz.explanation: 'Levantarse' is reflexive — the 'yo' form needs the pronoun 'me' before the verb.

quiz.question: Which is correct for 'Before eating, I shower'?
quiz.explanation: Use infinitive after 'antes de', and don't forget the reflexive pronoun 'me'.

quiz.question: How do you say 'I always eat with my family'?
quiz.explanation: Frequency adverb 'siempre' goes before the verb 'como'.

quiz.question: Arrange: acuesto / las once / me / a
quiz.explanation: Reflexive pronoun 'me' first, then verb 'acuesto', then time 'a las once'.
